content.text: The show is inspired by dark and experimental music I've heard over 6 years living here in Beijing, China, from DJ sets to live acts, noise and metal and weird ambient. The ways Chinese artists mix tunes, how they cater to young audiences hungry for odd music, and the explosion in Beijing in particular of black and doom metal as opposed to any other forms of more mainstream metal. The show will heavily feature Chinese artists. I will broadcast my mixes from my home in Beijing, China using my DJ controller and occasionally a microphone.

title: The Chuggernaut Hour with Chuggy Slowtis

content.text: Chuggy Slowtis was living and working happily as a Bavarian sheep herder, until a tragic accident that involved peeing on an electric fence caused such irreparable nerve damage, that meant his sheep herding days were over. (The real tragedy was that it was neither his pee, nor his electric fence, but that’s a story for another day.) Feeling utterly lost and in need of direction, his occupational therapist encouraged him to find a new hobby to help reinvigorate his zest for life. Chuggy took it upon himself to dig through the crates at his local record shop, and unearth records pressed at 45rpm, that sound good at 33rpm. His occupational therapist tried suggesting a slightly less ambitious hobby, but Chuggy would hear none of it. He had found his new calling as the king of wrong speeders (Der König der Falschraser) and is proud to share his finds with the good folk of É

DJ Egg is a music selector and artist who has been steadily rising and creating communities on the dancefloor through the Irish and UK scenes for the past seven years. Well-established in the jungle and bass scene, they have married their passion for bass-heavy music with their long-standing involvement in grassroots queer culture, developing a signature sound that encompasses euphoric queer house and club music into their sets. They co-run Dyke Nite (Limerick & Cork) and are the resident designer and frequent performer of Ar Ais Arís. 2025 found them playing multiple headline slots at parties such as Honeypot, Ar Ais Arís, Dublin Modular, Open Ear, All Together Now & Fuinneamh Festival.
A feisty selection of their butt shakin signature bass heavy sound. Pounding drums, vocals dreamy and twisted, and seductive grooves you can’t get enough of. What a BANGER.

Highlighting new independent music with a focus on the Irish scene, as well as upcoming gigs in the Munster area, and possibly the occasional interview or event report. Presented/produced by Emily Dollery.

Joyous Invasion has traveled by radio waves, birthed from the heart of the southwest desert, to Ireland. Burrowing transcripts of the soul from a time beyond in the hopes of corrupting the strait laced hive minds from the inside out. Dancing past their head chunks that fly in every which way.
THIS IS NOT A SAFE SPACE FOR SILENCE
, delivering a house mix that feels like a late-night decision you don’t regret but also don’t fully explain to anyone at work.
This is house music for people who’ve stared into the fridge at 2:47am and thought

Deep, techy, soulful, and occasionally unwell. The basslines lurk. The percussion judges you quietly. The grooves arrive uninvited and refuse to leave, like that friend who “just needs the couch for a bit.”
Expect tracks that sound expensive but emotionally unavailable. Drops that don’t drop — they
. Transitions smoother than a lie told with confidence. At times hypnotic. At times concerning. Always intentional.

No affirmations. No sunrise nonsense. No “hands in the air” unless you’re being questioned. Just relentless forward motion and the creeping realisation that this DJ has been doing this long enough to know exactly where your weak spots are.
Either way, something in your brain will recalibrate and you won’t be able to explain why.

content.text: The show is inspired by dark and experimental music I've heard over 6 years living here in Beijing, China, from DJ sets to live acts, noise and metal and weird ambient. The ways Chinese artists mix tunes, how they cater to young audiences hungry for odd music, and the explosion in Beijing in particular of black and doom metal as opposed to any other forms of more mainstream metal. The show will heavily feature Chinese artists. I will broadcast my mixes from my home in Beijing, China using my DJ controller and occasionally a microphone.

title: The Chuggernaut Hour with Chuggy Slowtis

content.text: huggy Slowtis was living and working happily as a Bavarian sheep herder, until a tragic accident that involved peeing on an electric fence caused such irreparable nerve damage, that meant his sheep herding days were over. (The real tragedy was that it was neither his pee, nor his electric fence, but that’s a story for another day.) Feeling utterly lost and in need of direction, his occupational therapist encouraged him to find a new hobby to help reinvigorate his zest for life. Chuggy took it upon himself to dig through the crates at his local record shop, and unearth records pressed at 45rpm, that sound good at 33rpm. His occupational therapist tried suggesting a slightly less ambitious hobby, but Chuggy would hear none of it. He had found his new calling as the king of wrong speeders (Der König der Falschraser) and is proud to share his finds with the good folk of É

title: Good Day Cork - Meeting the Archives

content.text: Documentary 1: 'Heritage Bazaar', explores the role of ethnic grocery shops in sustaining cultural identity.  This podcast is published by Good Day Cork. In 'Heritage Bazaar' invites the multi talented and Cork native, Daniel Heaphy to accept the challenge of making chai. Through the challenge, Daniel interviews various guests to explore the importance of having access to traditional ingredients and groceries and how this helps a person hold on to their cultural identity while living in a new country.

The documentary features interviews with Asad, owner of Asian Foods, Sravan Telu who moved to Ireland one year ago to study at MTU and Banu Rekha Balaji who has lived in Ireland for two decades, is an Occupational Therapist and founder of Therapix. The interviews were held on site at Asian Foods Cork and on North Main St.

Heritage Bazaar has been created with a cross border team with one of the team members, Mugdha Chemburkar Datay Co-Producer of Heritage Bazaar based in Brazil. Daniel Clancy is the Sound Recorder and Editor of the documentary and Music from Justin Grounds - both based in Ireland.

Joanna Dukkipati, founder Good Day Cork, received the Lord Mayor of Cork's Civic Award 2025 in recognition of cultural expression of migrant communities. The Heritage Bazaar documentary falls within this remit and also bridges majority communities in Ireland with the minorities through food memories.
This radio documentary is created with generous support from CESCA & Cork City Partnership – SICAP.
"The shortest distance between two people is a story."
Together with Cork-based organisation Think Speak Do Community Engagement, we launched a podcast on 30th July 2020 ie international Day of Friendship.

The podcast is called - 'Phone Pals' - where 2 strangers meet. This is Ep 1 featuring Lekha Menon Margassery & Tjitske de Vries. Both ladies live in Cork, Ireland & chat about their childhood, work, passions, creativity & motivations. Enjoy

content.text: Otherworld is a monthly folkore chat and music show, exploring tales from Ireland, Lithuania, and elsewhere.
This month, Šarūnė is joined by two guests, Ellen Flynn and Jake MM Griffin, who each bring a spooky folklore tale and a song for our listening pleasure.

Ellen Flynn is an ecologist from Cork who is currently doing a masters in sustainable development in Agri food and Co-operatives. She enjoys fish keeping and crochet. Folklore is something thats ingrained in her family history and she grew up hearing tales of fairies and the banshee.

Jake M.M. Griffin is a multidisciplinary artist from the Northside of Cork City. His writing has been published in Southword, The Quarryman, Flotsam, Abyss, Tower and CATU, among elsewhere. Jake is currently a student of English Literature at UCC.

radio show is where groove gets dressed up for the future. It’s a slick fusion of disco swagger, soulful warmth, and house precision — a sound that moves like honey but hits like caffeine.

: rubbery basslines, syncopated guitars, velvety Rhodes chords, and vocal hooks that sound like they were recorded on a Sunday afternoon but remixed under neon lights. The funk gives it that irresistible strut; the soul brings the emotion; the house framework keeps it tight, modern, and club-ready.
, every track is a reminder that rhythm and melody can still

. The drums snap with vintage charm, the synths glide with glossy restraint, and the vocal samples whisper stories of love, loss, and late-night redemption. It’s groove therapy — smooth enough for a cocktail bar, deep enough for a warehouse floor.
isn’t about showing off; it’s about
— to the dancefloor, to the moment, to that sweet spot where nostalgia and innovation lock eyes and start to move together."

Exploring so-called 'outside' singer songwriters. The work of these artists usually take the form of home recordings, field recordings or ramshackle studio recordings. They all present a unique voice in songwriting and could be classified nominally as folk and pop. Some examples of artists would be Connie Converse, Syd Barrett, Molly Drake, Daniel Johnston, John Fahey and plenty more.

Night Noises features music that feels different late at night. Selecting from different genres, Elinor explores music that captures the feeling of being alone in your bedroom after dark, when the world quiets down, and thoughts grow louder. It’s about solitude, stillness, and the sounds that keep you company when everything else is quiet.

Eoin is a Collector, Selector, and promoter based between London and Cork. Part of the  collective Offjazz that run irregular intimate gatherings in both cities. Jazz inspired sounds - Future Jazz, Acid Jazz, Downtempo, Broken Beat, Deep Disco, House, rare groove and on!

content.text: Barry Mullins is a visual artist, DJ, musician and avid Weather Watcher based in Doolin, County Clare.
"Tune in live to The Weather with Barry Mullins as I
play some of my records to soundtrack what’s going on outside and whats going on inside for you on a Friday morning over a nice coffee and chat."
From his house on a hill over looking the Aran Islands in Doolin, Barry also records his sibling show Sounds From a Hill.

An eclectic mix of music and mutterings, the show has gone on to influence his DJ sets drifting through genres at a whim. Barry is also a member of the DJ collective Sunday Spinners based in North West Clare.

title: 鬼子来了 Devils on the Doorstep

content.text: The show is inspired by dark and experimental music I've heard over 6 years living here in Beijing, China, from DJ sets to live acts, noise and metal and weird ambient. The ways Chinese artists mix tunes, how they cater to young audiences hungry for odd music, and the explosion in Beijing in particular of black and doom metal as opposed to any other forms of more mainstream metal. The show will heavily feature Chinese artists. I will broadcast my mixes from my home in Beijing, China using my DJ controller and occasionally a microphone.

title: The Chuggernaut Hour with Chuggy Slowtis

content.text: huggy Slowtis was living and working happily as a Bavarian sheep herder, until a tragic accident that involved peeing on an electric fence caused such irreparable nerve damage, that meant his sheep herding days were over. (The real tragedy was that it was neither his pee, nor his electric fence, but that’s a story for another day.) Feeling utterly lost and in need of direction, his occupational therapist encouraged him to find a new hobby to help reinvigorate his zest for life. Chuggy took it upon himself to dig through the crates at his local record shop, and unearth records pressed at 45rpm, that sound good at 33rpm. His occupational therapist tried suggesting a slightly less ambitious hobby, but Chuggy would hear none of it. He had found his new calling as the king of wrong speeders (Der König der Falschraser) and is proud to share his finds with the good folk of É

Afterglow is a curated exploration of experimental and unconventional music, drawing from a broad range of genres, including ambient, left-field bass, breaks, film scores and field recordings. By delving deep into my music collection, I aim to highlight different sonic textures and explore music that I don't typically get to play in clubs. I also intend on highlighting Irish and queer producers as part of my show. The focus will be on creating a thoughtful, immersive experience that encourages listeners to engage with the unusual and the unexplored.

What goes together better than music and zine making? Maybe a collaborative makers day with CZF and éist? Join us for an éist listening party and zine making session at the Triskel Sample Project Space from 12:00 until 15:00, where we will be listening live to Éist community radio and creating zines in response to the music and sounds. Participants will be given the opportunity to have their zines duplicated to add to the CZF Zine Library and have on display during the CZF Zine Library Showcase and Exhibition opening on the 25th of November. All materials will be provided, no previous experience needed. The workshop is free but spaces are limited and booking is required.

ChatGPT wrote the following ridiculous cheesy promo...

radio show isn’t just a set — it’s a spiritual workout wrapped in rhythm and groove. This sound digs deep into the roots of house, pulling from primal percussion, earthy chants, and hypnotic polyrhythms that feel older than language itself. It’s where ancient ritual meets modern circuitry.
The beats are alive — hand drums, congas, bongos, shakers — woven around warm basslines and shimmering synth textures. It’s music that feels
despite its digital bones. The tribal influence isn’t decoration; it’s the heartbeat of the set, driving energy forward while grounding it in something instinctual and communal.

, the tribal edge finds its balance in sleek production — metallic hats, spacious reverb, and soulful vocal cuts that echo like mantras. It’s a dancefloor sermon: organic meets electronic, groove meets grace. Each transition feels ceremonial, like a shift in the collective pulse of the room.
This is house stripped back to its essence — rhythm, connection, and spirit — but polished with modern finesse.
doesn’t just make you move; it makes you

soundcloud_match.description: For the past four decades, politician and activist Mick Barry has done things his way - informing his approach to politics, and life in general, has been a keen and wide-ranging interest in music of all sorts, from growing up listening to glam rock, punk and new wave, to a life spent among counter-cultural figures in Ireland and further afield.

title: C'mere to me with Noreen Murphy

content.text: I went to 'Prim's Bookshop-Bibliotherapy' in Kinsale and met the soundest people, Simon Prim, John Robb and Richard Jobson.
We talk about life, music, culture, writing books and selling them. Community, Punk, positivity, possibilities, home and and more.

mixcloud_match.name: Noreen Murphy. Richard Jobson, John Robb - C'meer to Me
mixcloud_match.description:
"Noreen travelled to Kinsale to meet bookseller Simon Prim and his guests, John Robb and Richard Jobson, who were performing a spoken-word event in Prim's Bookshop.
John  Robb is an English musician, author, music journalist, and media commentator. He is best known as the bassist and vocalist for the post-punk band The Membranes and as the frontman of the punk rock group Goldblade.

Robb is the founder, editor, and writer for the Louder Than War website and its accompanying monthly magazine. In addition to his journalism, he has authored several books on music and frequently appears in the media as a cultural and music commentator.

In 2014, he founded the Louder Than Words Festival, an annual celebration of music writing held in Manchester every November, where he continues to serve as executive director. Robb is also a TEDx speaker and a spoken word performer.

Richard Jobson is a Scottish musician, poet, filmmaker, writer, and producer, as well as a television presenter. He first ga

"Noreen travelled to Kinsale to meet bookseller Simon Prim and his guests, John Robb and Richard Jobson, who were performing a spoken-word event in Prim's Bookshop.
John  Robb is an English musician, author, music journalist, and media commentator. He is best known as the bassist and vocalist for the post-punk band The Membranes and as the frontman of the punk rock group Goldblade.

Robb is the founder, editor, and writer for the Louder Than War website and its accompanying monthly magazine. In addition to his journalism, he has authored several books on music and frequently appears in the media as a cultural and music commentator.

In 2014, he founded the Louder Than Words Festival, an annual celebration of music writing held in Manchester every November, where he continues to serve as executive director. Robb is also a TEDx speaker and a spoken word performer.

Richard Jobson is a Scottish musician, poet, filmmaker, writer, and producer, as well as a television presenter. He first gained recognition as the singer and songwriter for the punk and post-punk band Skids.

Simon Prim is the visionary behind Prim's Bookshop Bibliotherapy in Kinsale. A bookshop by day that morphs into a wine bar and cultural space in the evening. Originally from Youghal, Simon now calls Kinsale home."

content.text: Otherworld is a monthly folkore chat and music show, exploring tales from Ireland, Lithuania, and elsewhere.

This month, Šarūnė is joined by Kai Dranchak, an artist and creative producer based in Cork. Hailing from the US with proud Lithuanian roots, Kai brings his passion for folklore and some touching stories to this episode.

We discuss traditional Lithuanian folklore tales of Eglė Žalčių Karaliėnė (Egle the Queen of Serpents), Jūratė and Kastytis (Story of the origin of amber), familial folk stories, as well as our favourite folk and Lithuanian tunes.

content.text: Progressive House for an Educated Ear with Kevin Lord.
ChatGPT wrote the following waffle for me.....it's hilarious B.S. !

progressive house mix isn’t just a playlist of thumping kicks and hypnotic synths — it’s a masterclass in restraint, flow, and evolution. This kind of mix isn’t for casual listeners looking for instant drops; it’s for those who appreciate the architecture of sound, the slow burn of tension, and the emotional nuance tucked between transitions.
Each track is chosen with intent — not just to

something in context. It might start minimal, with deep pads and a patient groove, then bloom into lush melodic layers that glide rather than crash. The percussion stays tight and tactile, giving motion without clutter. The bassline doesn’t dominate; it
, pulsing like a heartbeat guiding the listener through shifting moods.

An educated ear hears the subtle cues — the way a hi-hat opens a fraction wider before a break, how a filtered reverb tail teases what’s coming next, how the DJ lets a track’s story finish rather than cutting it short for another “moment.” It’s progression as philosophy, not just genre: each track builds upon the last, crafting a seamless journey from introspection to elevation.

In short, a true progressive house mix for the educated ear doesn’t just move the body — it seduces the brain, rewards patience, and turns listening into immersion. It’s a conversation between artist and audience, told in frequencies instead of words."

Night Noises features music that feels different late at night. Selecting from different genres, Elinor explores music that captures the feeling of being alone in your bedroom after dark, when the world quiets down, and thoughts grow louder. It’s about solitude, stillness, and the sounds that keep you company when everything else is quiet.

Eoin is a Collector, Selector, and promoter based between London and Cork. Part of the  collective Offjazz that run irregular intimate gatherings in both cities. Jazz inspired sounds - Future Jazz, Acid Jazz, Downtempo, Broken Beat, Deep Disco, House, rare groove and on!

The themes include migration, incarceration, sovereignty, neutrality and territory through the lens of feminist accelerationism. Each show will be a minimalist audio essay, featuring sampled voices presented alongside tracks with room for contemplation.

Your fortnightly mix of global dance music cooked up in a cottage on the shores of Garrettstown, Co Cork.
Today's DOOMSDAY special edition commemorates Daniel Dumile, AKA MF DOOM, Viktor Vaughn and King Geedorah, who died on 31 October 2020.

Known for his mischievous personas, unique flow, rhymes and beat making, obscure taped clips from TV, not to mention some epic collaborations with likes of Madlib, Dangermouse, and Czarface, among many others, he left an unmistakable imprint on global hiphop.

Dumile's career was marked by tragedy, with the death of his brother and fellow KMD member in his early career, then  losing his son of 14 just a few years before his own passing. I think this was a powerful influence on retreat from hiphop in the late 90s-early 2000s, then reinvention as MF DOOM . Much like the comic character himself, he suffered a personal tragedy and reemerged as an evil character to terrorise the world's population. In DOOM's case, he terrorised the hiphop community with a style and flow unmatched during his pomp.

Among all his qualities, it is the rogue nature that I always loved about DOOM''s various alter egos. It comes out in his rhymes and story telling, sometimes a sinister baddie, other times a bumbling, shady dude you'd run into on the street. As an underground icon he played this up - to the degree that I've heard stories of him sending an imposter to play his shows during the height of his popularity. And while I think I'd be pretty gutted if I was one of those punters.. you also gotta love the mythology of it all..
RIP Daniel Dumile, AKA MF DOOM, AKA Victor Vaughn, AKA King Geedorah.
MF DOOM - Doom, Are You Awake? (Skit)

Sampa The Great - Rhymes To The East
Pusha T - Numbers On The Boards
Czarface & MF DOOM ft. Open Mike Eagle & Kendra Morris - Phantoms
MF DOOM ft. Mr Fantastik - Rapp Snitch Knishes

content.text: gorsefireradio is a fortnightly show on Éist dedicated to mood driven and theme focused sonic journeys.
Oisín and James use storytelling and detailed curation to guide listeners through a musical expedition that spans genres from folk, electronica & trip-hop to death metal and beyond.

content.text: Barry Mullins is a visual artist, DJ, musician and avid Weather Watcher based in Doolin, County Clare.
"Tune in live to The Weather with Barry Mullins as I
play some of my records to soundtrack what’s going on outside and whats going on inside for you on a Friday morning over a nice coffee and chat."
From his house on a hill over looking the Aran Islands in Doolin, Barry also records his sibling show Sounds From a Hill.

An eclectic mix of music and mutterings, the show has gone on to influence his DJ sets drifting through genres at a whim. Barry is also a member of the DJ collective Sunday Spinners based in North West Clare.
Catch up on recent éist shows broadcast through the night.

title: 鬼子来了 Devils on the Doorstep

content.text: The show is inspired by dark and experimental music I've heard over 6 years living here in Beijing, China, from DJ sets to live acts, noise and metal and weird ambient. The ways Chinese artists mix tunes, how they cater to young audiences hungry for odd music, and the explosion in Beijing in particular of black and doom metal as opposed to any other forms of more mainstream metal. The show will heavily feature Chinese artists. I will broadcast my mixes from my home in Beijing, China using my DJ controller and occasionally a microphone.

mixcloud_match.name: Numbertheory w/ thruoutin- 鬼子来了 Devils on the Doorstep (30/10/2025)
mixcloud_match.description:
This month we have a guest on the show - thruoutin

His mix is a loose collection of ambient, experimental electronic, and post-rock adjacent music that thruoutin selected for the Devils on the Doorstep show. All artists are based in China. 

thruoutin is an electronic musician, DJ, and producer who has lived in China for fifteen years. He is also the founder of Seippelabel. His music draws on a wide range of influences, combining tender yet distinctive electronic textures with elements of traditional Chinese music to create a unique personal aesthetic.

title: The Chuggernaut Hour with Chuggy Slowtis

content.text: huggy Slowtis was living and working happily as a Bavarian sheep herder, until a tragic accident that involved peeing on an electric fence caused such irreparable nerve damage, that meant his sheep herding days were over. (The real tragedy was that it was neither his pee, nor his electric fence, but that’s a story for another day.) Feeling utterly lost and in need of direction, his occupational therapist encouraged him to find a new hobby to help reinvigorate his zest for life. Chuggy took it upon himself to dig through the crates at his local record shop, and unearth records pressed at 45rpm, that sound good at 33rpm. His occupational therapist tried suggesting a slightly less ambitious hobby, but Chuggy would hear none of it. He had found his new calling as the king of wrong speeders (Der König der Falschraser) and is proud to share his finds with the good folk of É

Afterglow is a curated exploration of experimental and unconventional music, drawing from a broad range of genres, including ambient, left-field bass, breaks, film scores and field recordings. By delving deep into my music collection, I aim to highlight different sonic textures and explore music that I don't typically get to play in clubs. I also intend on highlighting Irish and queer producers as part of my show. The focus will be on creating a thoughtful, immersive experience that encourages listeners to engage with the unusual and the unexplored.

mixcloud_match.name: Ellen O'Keeffe - Our Farm - Beetle, Bramble and Blackbird: Near-by Nature 26.10.25
mixcloud_match.description:
I had a lovely chat with John Murphy, a farmer in East Cork, for this month's Éist radio show, with ssome music mixed in. John is one of a group of people who have come together to create the Our Farm project. This is a community farm that aims to work for the farmers, the community, and nature. It is based on a model of community based, sustainable farming coming out of the Netherlands called Herenboeren. It was truly wonderful to talk to John chat about this. He is so knowledgeable about farming, nature, and the challenges facing us all in a broken, unequal food system. He is also full of infectious enthusiasm about a better food future for all. Our Farm is looking for new members for 2026.

If you are interested in food security, farming for nature, community resilience, and future visions for a fair agricultural system in Ireland, have a listen.

content.text: This month acid dave invites darkmavis.

"A club powerhouse in Ireland, and beyond. Mark closed out one of our parties back in March, and it was WILD. Still one of my favourite dancefloor moments from this year. He's a key member of iconic kink rave Joyride, boss of label Ethereal Skies, a regular fixture in London's queer party scene, and all-around lovely guy :)
He brings us on a winding immersive journey of shimmering ambient, deep house, euphoric trance, and dreamy breaks. One to sit back for, slow down and get lost in."

Transcontinental is a music show focusing primarily on black music, and afro-diasporic stories. The show aims to offer a glimpse into an era, a country; to place an artist and music in context.

mixcloud_match.name: Caroline Mudingo Dipanda - 251018-Transcontinental
mixcloud_match.description:
Transcontinental- Episode 8

Sarah Lubala is a Congolese-born poet, who relocated to South Africa. Her debut collection, 'A History of Disappearance' traces her experience as a Congolese-born immigrant. It is a beautifully written collection of poetry addressing displacement , mental illness, memory, pain, violence, sexual violence.

In this episode, Sarah Lubala reads excerpts from her essay entitled 'First Country'. She touches on the longing for her mother country, the Democratic Republic of Congo; the genocide in Congo; Congolese women's reproductive rights, and her love for the women in her life.

✹Laos Music Special✹
A collection of music from three CD collections by the Archives of Traditional Music in Laos. With thanks to Ton Souvannalith for his help with translating the text.

Tracklist:

Calling the Elephant, Laos: Instrumental Music In Laos (IMIL) (Selection I), instruments: sanai, recorded Champasak, 31 November, 2000.

Seub Tang Waai (Tang Waai Suite), Traditional Lao Song, instruments: 3 x 8 khaen, gajab pii, 2 pii, saw oo, gong hang, shing, recorded Vientiane, 14 September, 2003.

Lam phau Nge, Laos: IMIL (Selection III), instruments: khap, lanat ek, lanat thum lek, kong ping, so-hep, xap, so-u, recorded Phonpheng 23 April, 2001.

Samakkhydeuancheng, Laos: IMIL (Selection III), instruments: 5 kong pe prai (khong chum), kong nong neng (5 khong la), recorded Vongsomphen 31 March, 2001.

Lai Mae Haang Gom Luk (Mother Cradle Rocking Child Melody), Traditional Lao Song, instruments: 8 khaen, recorded Vientiane, 14 September, 2003.

Khmu Epic Folksong, Laos: IMIL (Selection I), instruments: khui Khmu, recorded Luang Prabang, 1988.

Hmong Lay Folksong “Khithot phusao”, Laos: IMIL (Selection I), instruments: pi Hmong, recorded Sam Neua 12 January, 2000.

Introduction to the Ramayana Ballet “Pheng Kaunau”, Laos: IMIL (Selection I), instruments: lanat ek, lanat thum, khongvong, khonvong nyai, kong taphong, sing, recorded Luang Prabang, 1974.

Ceremonial Music at Wat Phou “Pheng Kom”, Laos: IMIL (Selection I), instruments: lanat ek, khongvong, pikeo, khui lip, lanat thum lek, kong that, kong taphong, 2 khong nyai, recorded Wat Phou, 19 February, 2000.

Lao Folkdance from Sukhumma, Laos: IMIL (Selection I), instruments: khen, kong tum, so-u, kachappi, recorded Champasak, 18 February, 2000.

Lam Long Phone Does Giao, Traditional Lao Song, instruments: 4 x 8 khaen, gajab pii, saw oo, gong hang, shing, recorded Vientiane, 14 September, 2003.

Lam kaloey, Laos: IMIL (Selection III), instruments: lam, khen 7, recorded Thetsaban, 27 March, 2001.

Lao Khen Solo “Lotfray Taylang”, Laos: IMIL (Selection I), instruments: khen, recorded Vientiane, 1986.

Sepluammu Changvaxeung Bangfai, Laos: IMIL (Selection I), instruments: kong thum, kong hang, sanai, seng, phang hat, recorded Savannakhet, 27 July, 2000.

Lam xieng, Laos: IMIL (Selection III), instruments: lam, khen 7, kong Kado, seng, recorded Thetsaban, 28 March, 2001.

Lai Phu Sao Yik Mae (Young Woman Missing Mother Melody), Traditional Lao Song, instruments: 8 khaen, recorded Vientiane 14 September, 2003.

Lao Folkdance “Khonsavan”, Laos: IMIL (Selection I), instruments: kachappi, khen, recorded Kengkok, 26 July, 2000.

content.text: Otherworld is a monthly folkore chat and music show, exploring tales from Ireland, Lithuania, and elsewhere.
This month, Šarūnė is joined by two guests, Ellen Flynn and Jake MM Griffin, who each bring a spooky folklore tale and a song for our listening pleasure.

Ellen Flynn is an ecologist from Cork who is currently doing a masters in sustainable development in Agri food and Co-operatives. She enjoys fish keeping and crochet. Folklore is something thats ingrained in her family history and she grew up hearing tales of fairies and the banshee.

Jake M.M. Griffin is a multidisciplinary artist from the Northside of Cork City. His writing has been published in Southword, The Quarryman, Flotsam, Abyss, Tower and CATU, among elsewhere. Jake is currently a student of English Literature at UCC.

content.text: Hi Tech Soul - A forthrightly 2 hour pre-recorded DJ mix with Myself Cormac See.
This episode is a game of 2 halves.
The 1st hour is deep house vibes.
2nd Hour is straight up 5 card stud house music four 2 the floor.
A little A-Team intro & divider to make it stand out and also to label for myself so I remember what mix it is.
Wanna hear & see this in person ? Catch me regularly in Edison Bar Washington Street Cork where I have been doing this for the last 15 years.

Exploring so-called 'outside' singer songwriters. The work of these artists usually take the form of home recordings, field recordings or ramshackle studio recordings. They all present a unique voice in songwriting and could be classified nominally as folk and pop. Some examples of artists would be Connie Converse, Syd Barrett, Molly Drake, Daniel Johnston, John Fahey and plenty more.
A monthly hour of music for a headrush and a heartrush. From folk, art pop, alt rock, and post punk, to soul, jazz and funk. A mood led soundscapes to start your day.

content.text: is all about shoegaze and dream pop, from the genres' beginnings (if there was one) to the present day. Shoegaze is "the scene that celebrates itself", and

Escape to Erewhon is a show that explores escapology through voice samples mixed with music from the sub-bass genres and experimental fields. The themes include migration, incarceration, sovereignty, neutrality and territory through the lens of feminist accelerationism. Each show will be a minimalist audio essay, featuring sampled voices presented alongside tracks with room for contemplation.
THE ECLECTIC is a show about taking songs & sounds out of their context, and then putting them back in.

content.text: Barry Mullins is a visual artist, DJ, musician and avid Weather Watcher based in Doolin, County Clare.
"Tune in live to The Weather with Barry Mullins as I
play some of my records to soundtrack what’s going on outside and whats going on inside for you on a Friday morning over a nice coffee and chat."
From his house on a hill over looking the Aran Islands in Doolin, Barry also records his sibling show Sounds From a Hill.

An eclectic mix of music and mutterings, the show has gone on to influence his DJ sets drifting through genres at a whim. Barry is also a member of the DJ collective Sunday Spinners based in North West Clare.

title: 鬼子来了 Devils on the Doorstep

content.text: The show is inspired by dark and experimental music I've heard over 6 years living here in Beijing, China, from DJ sets to live acts, noise and metal and weird ambient. The ways Chinese artists mix tunes, how they cater to young audiences hungry for odd music, and the explosion in Beijing in particular of black and doom metal as opposed to any other forms of more mainstream metal. The show will heavily feature Chinese artists. I will broadcast my mixes from my home in Beijing, China using my DJ controller and occasionally a microphone.

title: The Chuggernaut Hour with Chuggy Slowtis

content.text: Chuggy Slowtis was living and working happily as a Bavarian sheep herder, until a tragic accident that involved peeing on an electric fence caused such irreparable nerve damage, that meant his sheep herding days were over. (The real tragedy was that it was neither his pee, nor his electric fence, but that’s a story for another day.) Feeling utterly lost and in need of direction, his occupational therapist encouraged him to find a new hobby to help reinvigorate his zest for life. Chuggy took it upon himself to dig through the crates at his local record shop, and unearth records pressed at 45rpm, that sound good at 33rpm. His occupational therapist tried suggesting a slightly less ambitious hobby, but Chuggy would hear none of it. He had found his new calling as the king of wrong speeders (Der König der Falschraser) and is proud to share his finds with the good folk of É

content.text: My show will be a curated exploration of experimental and unconventional music, drawing from a broad range of genres, including ambient, left-field bass, breaks, film scores and field recordings. By delving deep into my music collection, I aim to highlight different sonic textures and explore music that I don't typically get to play in clubs. I also intend on highlighting Irish and queer producers as part of my show. The focus will be on creating a thoughtful, immersive experience that encourages listeners to engage with the unusual and the unexplored.

Thanks Jenny for taking the time to talk to me and the Rev. Abigail Sines for providing the space.

content.text: As always, I'll take you on a roller coaster through diverse genres, filled with emotions! Get set to GROOVE and let the beats take you on an unforgettable and psychedelics journey 🚀

is an ongoing body of work about the Queer rural experience with specific focus on isolation and loneliness. What is central to this is the idea of betrayal. By this I mean how looking back at Irish Queer history we can see a distinct move towards assimilation, normalisation and financialisation of Queer identity.

What I have put together for this show is a soundscape of different voices singing, humming and screaming. The starting point for these recordings is the tradition of Keening but over time is now more aligned with Zeynep Bulut’s ideas of voicing. While making the recordings, each of the participants mentioned how cathartic the exercises felt, and I link this back to Anne Carson’s writings on the gender of the voice and questioning of who has the right to be heard. Mixed in are some fictional pieces I am working on that are written through a country boys make do  methodology when it comes to sex, stability and re-ruralisation or counter-urbanisation.

content.text: Episode #32 of Súgradh.  No club listings and links this week. Uninterrupted. Stay safe, have a great weekend and enjoy!

Pedro's monthly show Farofada is more than just a name – it’s a celebration of mix and blend. In Brazil, farofa is a beloved dish found from north to south, but in Rio de Janeiro, the word also doubles as slang for something chaotic, messy, or working-class. And that’s exactly the spirit behind this show – embracing the full spectrum, from the refined to the raw.

Farofada dives deep into the rich diversity of Brazilian music – from rhythms born in the favelas and outskirts, to sounds that climbed hills, crossed oceans, and resonated around the globe. Rooted traditions meet modern fusions, always seasoned with unmistakable Brazilian flavor.
Get ready to hear a bit of everything – with heart, soul, and joyful disorder. Because in the end, everything becomes farofa. And that’s a compliment

𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐛𝐞𝐚𝐧 𝐕𝐨𝐲𝐚𝐠𝐞: 𝐄𝐩𝐢𝐬𝐨𝐝𝐞 𝟖 𝐢𝐬 𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞!
This episode is a throwback to the vibrant musical pace of the 𝐂𝐫𝐞𝐨𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐡𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐛𝐞𝐚𝐧. 
We're celebrating the legends who pioneered sounds in the 𝟕𝟎𝐬, from g𝐫𝐨𝐨𝐯𝐲 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐫𝐡𝐲𝐭𝐡𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐂𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 to soulful 𝐊𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐚.Then a bit of 𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐛𝐞𝐚𝐧 𝐉𝐚𝐳𝐳 & some 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐜𝐮𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 create each of them an organic there and there within these two hours.
If you miss the live show, you can catch the full recording and playlist on my socials right after the broadcast.

Tune in this Thursday, August 14th, 2025 at these times:
* 𝟕 𝐏𝐌 in Ireland and the UK
* 𝟐 𝐏𝐌 in Guadeloupe, Martinique, St. Maarten, Miami, New York, and Toronto
* 𝟑 𝐏𝐌 in São Paulo and Cayenne
* 𝟔 𝐏𝐌 in Accra
* 𝟖 𝐏𝐌 in Barcelona, Paris, Berlin
I hope you enjoy this journey through Caribbean music!

Playlist available via https://serato.com/playlists/karuk/caribbean-voyage-8

content.text: Exploring so-called 'outside' singer songwriters. The work of these artists usually take the form of home recordings, field recordings or ramshackle studio recordings. They all present a unique voice in songwriting and could be classified nominally as folk and pop. Some examples of artists would be Connie Converse, Syd Barrett, Molly Drake, Daniel Johnston, John Fahey and plenty more.
Catch up with repeats of recent episodes.

Night Noises features music that feels different late at night. Selecting from different genres, Elinor explores music that captures the feeling of being alone in your bedroom after dark, when the world quiets down, and thoughts grow louder. It’s about solitude, stillness, and the sounds that keep you company when everything else is quiet.
Negative experiments, Ireland. scrap/ditch/groan. trades welcomed. Nazis fuck off

content.text: Escape to Erewhon is a show that explores escapology through voice samples mixed with music from the sub-bass genres and experimental fields. The themes include migration, incarceration, sovereignty, neutrality and territory through the lens of feminist accelerationism. Each show will be a minimalist audio essay, featuring sampled voices presented alongside tracks with room for contemplation.

content.text: Barry Mullins is a visual artist, DJ, musician and avid Weather Watcher based in Doolin, County Clare.
"Tune in live to The Weather with Barry Mullins as I
play some of my records to soundtrack what’s going on outside and whats going on inside for you on a Friday morning over a nice coffee and chat."
From his house on a hill over looking the Aran Islands in Doolin, Barry also records his sibling show Sounds From a Hill.

An eclectic mix of music and mutterings, the show has gone on to influence his DJ sets drifting through genres at a whim. Barry is also a member of the DJ collective Sunday Spinners based in North West Clare.

title: 鬼子来了 Devils on the Doorstep

content.text: The show is inspired by dark and experimental music I've heard over 6 years living here in Beijing, China, from DJ sets to live acts, noise and metal and weird ambient. The ways Chinese artists mix tunes, how they cater to young audiences hungry for odd music, and the explosion in Beijing in particular of black and doom metal as opposed to any other forms of more mainstream metal.

content.text: THE CYCLICAL NATURE OF FEEDBACK LOOPS.
A TAPE SENT BACK AND FORTH BETWEEN FIONNUALA BLOEM DORAN AND SAMUEL JULIAN GRACE. EACH MONTH RECORDED ON TOP OF THE PREVIOUS MONTH USING NO-INPUT MIXING, DIGITAL SOFTWARES AND TAPE LOOPS.
BY FIONNUALA BLOEM DORAN AND SAMUEL JULIAN GRACE.

An educational show spreading the good word and tunes of the Javanese Gamelan and how it ended up in Cork. Exploring traditional pieces and modern compositions. Including how the gamelan influenced Debussy all the way to featuring in The Hobbit: Desolation of Smaug.
To hear about performances and workshops follow Cork Community Gamelan on Instagram.
THE ECLECTIC is a show about taking songs & sounds out of their context, and then putting them back in.

Afterglow is a curated exploration of experimental and unconventional music, drawing from a broad range of genres, including left-field club, bass, breaks, ambient, film scores and field recordings. This show highlights different sonic textures and explores music that I don't typically get to play in clubs, while spotlighting both Irish and queer producers. Afterglow focuses on creating a thoughtful, immersive experience that encourages listeners to engage with the unusual and the unexplored.

Expect unexpected connections, odd facts and, always, a deep love for collective learning and conscious listening.
In episode 1, they tackle Thomas Kivney's gay sci-fi rom-com Max And Tony's Epic One-Night Stand. Script available at the following link if you'd like to read along from home:
This first episode is a dreamy soup of tunes, partially inspired by a bizarre dream book. Expect lots of ambient tunes and a few bits in between.

content.text: Today, my show is being given over to the Save Our Sperrins Podcast with Marella Fyffe and  V’cenza Cirefice.

V’cenza has been a long-time supporter of the Save Our Sperrins campaign, bringing her energy, creativity, and care to our struggle. In this conversation we talk about her work with counter-mapping and visual storytelling – powerful tools that help communities see what’s at stake and find new ways to resist extractivism.
This is a really insightful listen for anyone facing similar threats in their own place.
To find out more, below are some useful links.

content.text: Joyous Invasion has traveled by radio waves, birthed from the heart of the southwest desert, to Ireland. Burrowing transcripts of the soul from a time beyond in the hopes of corrupting the strait laced hive minds from the inside out. Dancing past their head chunks that fly in every which way. From the obsessive passions of a POC (Mexican/Zuni/Caddo/Apache/Navajo/Hispanic) New Mexican Burqueña, cartoonist, and filmmaker who hopes to build a community of inclusivity and shared knowledge; a joyous invasion!
Terry Riley – A Rainbow in Curved Air
A monthly Éist-exclusive special from the heads behind the Celtic Ligers comedy/politics podcast, focusing on in-depth interviews and conversations.

For the past four decades, politician and activist Mick Barry has done things his way - informing his approach to politics, and life in general, has been a keen and wide-ranging interest in music of all sorts, from growing up listening to glam rock, punk and new wave, to a life spent among counter-cultural figures in Ireland and further afield.

soundcloud_match.title: Spirit Level 03 // Radio Friendly with Stevie G
soundcloud_match.description: Spirit Level Episode 03 captures a conversational documentary with Stevie G of Radio Friendly. Spirit Level is a radio show getting to know community radio stations around the world through conversation. In addition to learning how, where and why community radio stations exist, we're sharing what we are listening to - an exchange of all forms audio capturing our attention on the day.

content.text: This celebratory mix of Optimo (Espacio) and particularly Keith (aka JD Twitch's legacy could've been 10 hours long, such is the length and breadth of great music Keith has turned me on to. Whole strata of underground music were first brought to my ears in sweaty nights at Optimo's legendary Sunday night residency at the Sub Club in Glasgow. Even beyond the club nights, the Optimo Music label often drains my Bandcamp funds with it's incredible roster of talent.
May Jah speed Keith and Johnny for the excellent work they do.

content.text: Fort Evil Fruit has been releasing a diverse catalogue of broadly "experimental" music from Ireland and around the world since 2011.

Transcontinental is a music show focusing primarily on black music, and afro-diasporic stories. The show aims to offer a glimpse into an era, a country; to place an artist and music in context.

mixcloud_match.name: Caroline Mudingo Dipanda - Transcontinental: Authentically Plastic
mixcloud_match.description:
Authentically Plastic is a dazzling Ugandan DJ, producer and the co-founder of the queer collective ANTI-MASS. 

In this episode we discuss their very first album, 'Raw space', which was released on the label Hakuna Kulala; a label run by Rey Sapienz and the founders of the label Nyege Nyege Tapes.

'Raw Space' is an interesting, intricate, innovative album, at time disorienting. 

Authentically Plastic offers a glimpse into Kampala's electronic music scene and arts scene - community-driven infrastructures, Hakuna Kulala's founder Rey Sapienz, Nyege Nyege Festival.

They share a candid account of their artistic journey in a context where the 2023 Anti-Homosexuality Act ushered in homophobic and transphobic assaults - a celebration of East African music, and queer joy.

Franz Aembient the mastermind and subculturalist of Kashual Plastik. A protagonist of the vibrant Neukölln scene, influential disc jockey and selector. Program Curator of arkaoda Berlin. He works out of Berlin and is involved in various music/art projects under several dozen mysterious names. 

With his own label Kashual Plastik, he experiments with an authentic DIY approach composed of an intriguing graphic aesthetic and a hard-to-categorize sound inspired by Punk, 80s Synth-wave, EBM, Electro as well as Noise, Psychedelic & Garage Rock, Ambient, Folk and Leftfield electronics.

Bloomers, Enid Conway & Kim Crowley, in conversation with founder of Dlúthpháirtíocht, Seán Óg Ó Mhurchú & artist St Diabhail. The exhibition Dlúthphaírtíocht is at Laneway Gallery Cork until August 17th.
The exhibition aims to highlight the historical links between Ireland & Palestine as well as exhibiting contemporary responses to what is unfolding in the occupied territories.

Most importantly, Dlúthpháirtíocht: Irish Solidarity with Palestine is a form of direct action. It is a way that you can interact, learn, and contribute financially to the project, with all profits being donated to Dignity for Palestinians, a charity run by UNRWA doctor Musallam Abukhalil, on the ground in central Gaza.

soundcloud_match.title: Bloomers x Dluthphairtiocht
soundcloud_match.description:
Bloomers sat down to chat with Seán Óg Ó Murchú and St Diabhal about Dlúthpháirtíocht.

Dlúthpháirtíocht, translated as 'solidarity' as Gaeilge, is a nonprofit, multidisciplinary art collective, primarily consisting of Irish and Palestinian artists. Beginning as a poem, the project grew into an online publication, and then an exhibition in London. The exhibition is now touring Ireland and is currently on in the Laneway Gallery, Shandon Street, Cork until the 17th August.

The exhibition aims to highlight the historical links between Ireland & Palestine as well as exhibiting contemporary responses to what is unfolding in the occupied territories.

Dlúthpháirtíocht is a form of direct action. It is a way that you can interact, learn, and contribute financially to the project, with all profits being donated to Dignity for Palestinians, a charity run by UNRWA doctor Musallam Abukhalil, on the ground in central Gaza.

content.text: Pedro's monthly show Farofada is more than just a name – it’s a celebration of mix and blend. In Brazil, farofa is a beloved dish found from north to south, but in Rio de Janeiro, the word also doubles as slang for something chaotic, messy, or working-class. And that’s exactly the spirit behind this show – embracing the full spectrum, from the refined to the raw.

Farofada dives deep into the rich diversity of Brazilian music – from rhythms born in the favelas and outskirts, to sounds that climbed hills, crossed oceans, and resonated around the globe. Rooted traditions meet modern fusions, always seasoned with unmistakable Brazilian flavor.
Get ready to hear a bit of everything – with heart, soul, and joyful disorder. Because in the end, everything becomes farofa. And that’s a compliment

content.text: We're kicking things off with some contemporary jazz, a perfect way to ease into the groove.
After that, we're throwing it back to the early 2000s and 2010s with some classic Reggae Dancehall, celebrating the influential sounds that defined an era.
From there, we'll move into the present with some vibrant Shatta, before giving a massive shout-out to the incredible musical contributions from the Anglophone islands of the Lesser Antilles.
Get ready for a high-energy mix of
Big ups to the scenes of 𝐒𝐨𝐜𝐚
With the 𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝗡𝗼𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗛𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗻𝗶𝘃𝗮𝗹 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱 just around the corner in late August, this episode is the perfect way to get in the spirit.

Don't worry if you miss the live show, you'll find the full recordings on my socials (MixCloud & SoundCloud) a bit after the online broadcast, with the playlist available on my Mixcloud and Serato, in particular.

content.text: Exploring so-called 'outside' singer songwriters. The work of these artists usually take the form of home recordings, field recordings or ramshackle studio recordings. They all present a unique voice in songwriting and could be classified nominally as folk and pop. Some examples of artists would be Connie Converse, Syd Barrett, Molly Drake, Daniel Johnston, John Fahey and plenty more.

content.text: Each month, me and one of my friends discuss our relationship with each other through music. Most of my relationships have music as a core component; and I think it’s an interesting vessel to navigate our relationships through - what we first connected over, artists we’ve put each other onto over the years, core memories, and even some recommendations today we both think the other isnt listening to.
THE ECLECTIC is a show about taking songs & sounds out of their context, and then putting them back in.

content.text: Barry Mullins is a visual artist, DJ, musician and avid Weather Watcher based in Doolin, County Clare.
"Tune in live to The Weather with Barry Mullins as I
play some of my records to soundtrack what’s going on outside and whats going on inside for you on a Friday morning over a nice coffee and chat."
From his house on a hill over looking the Aran Islands in Doolin, Barry also records his sibling show Sounds From a Hill.

An eclectic mix of music and mutterings, the show has gone on to influence his DJ sets drifting through genres at a whim. Barry is also a member of the DJ collective Sunday Spinners based in North West Clare.
Wake up and catch the bird!

content.text: I spoke to Muireann Ni Beathain from The Connolly Youth Movement about the work they are doing with the Cuban Solidarity Forum of Ireland.

We talk about Cuba. The Connolly Youth Movement. The Cuban Solidarity Forum. Charity/Solidarity. Life for young people in Ireland. Unwelcoming political spaces for women. Tax havens.  Housing. The cost of living. Organising. Housing. Global finance.  And more....
Chat recorded at the offices of The Independent Workers Union in Cork.

content.text: Joyous Invasion has traveled by radio waves, birthed from the heart of the southwest desert, to Ireland. Burrowing transcripts of the soul from a time beyond in the hopes of corrupting the strait laced hive minds from the inside out. Dancing past their head chunks that fly in every which way. From the obsessive passions of a POC (Mexican/Zuni/Caddo/Apache/Navajo/Hispanic) New Mexican Burqueña, cartoonist, and filmmaker who hopes to build a community of inclusivity and shared knowledge; a joyous invasion!
A four-part series featuring stories and sounds of the Irish landscape as well as folk music from Ireland and further afield.

content.text: THE ECLECTIC is a show about taking songs & sounds out of their context, and then putting them back in.

That Got Me To Hear is a whatsapp music sharing project started by Ronan Leonard, where a whatsapp group opens for an hour and people share music and respond to other choices. It opens a few times a week with no regularity. Using his son's DnD dice, Ronan picks a random one and builds an hour long radio show from it.

A monthly show from Alannah Dunford - co-runner of Galway’s esteemed club night and record label, Ar Ais Arís. Delving into the world of electronic music in all its shapes and forms. With a focus on synthesizers and the key female figures of electronic music who have embraced technology and redefined how we listen to and produce music today.

content.text: I caught up with Emmet Green and American band, All Seeing Eyes. There's some great chat and music from the new album, "Trone des Fleur".

All-Seeing Eyes were born from a party. Originally formed as a one off party band, they continue to carry that mantle to this day. They have shared stages with the Detroit Cobras, Tav Falco and Panther Burns, John Sinclair, Low Cut Connie and Cedric Burnside. Mixing genres that are typically disparate, such as blues, psych, classic rock and jazz, Thee All-Seeing Eyes have kept audiences on edge by taking unorthodox approaches that are unpredictable and exploratory.

Hailing from the small river town of Dayton KY, the 3 multi-instrumentalists have crafted their new record “Trone des Fleur” (Masonic Records) and are ready to present it to the world. The tracks were all arranged, recorded, and produced by the band at the studio that they built by hand, Masonic Sounds Studio B.

Johnny Walker is a board certified psychiatrist specializing in psychosis and mania. This bleeds through into his guitar playing, as he believes playing live music is a form of therapy for the masses. Being Soledad Brother #1, he has played his own brand of psych/blues/rock for hundreds of thousands of music fans. Dr Walker has played all the slide guitar on the White Stripes self titled first album. He sang for the MC5 on their first European tour in 30 years in 2004. He even backed up John Sinclair in numerous shows while the Chief read prose about blues guitar players. Johnny was asked to reform the Detroit Cobras, playing bass for them while in medical school. He has engaged in many recording sessions including The Greenhornes, Low Cut Connie and too many more to list here. Johnny’s band the Soledad Brothers, was noted to be John Peel's favorite band, per John’s son. Mr Peel gave them the honor of being the first track on his only curated compilation, Fabric Live, serving as bookends for the comp with the Undertones. That is high praise, indeed.  Third Man Records (Jack White's record label) will also be releasing all the back catalog of Soledad Brothers material in the near future.

Lance Kaufman is a drummer and vocalist extraordinaire. He has fronted traditional rock billy band, the Star Devils for decades. He was the principle engineer for the new Thee All-Seeing Eyes album as well. While holding down the bass duties in the band, Kane Kitchen has done dozens of sessions at Masonic Sounds Studios and toured extensively with outlaw country star Jeremy Pinnell. He has displayed the ability to adapt to numerous genres of music and is a great arranger of songs.
Jack White: “Johnny Walker is one of the great ones. A true philistine of rock and roll”

content.text: LET'S WORSHIP BRIAN WILSON AND SLY STONE Y'ALL. Both paved new paths in their respective genres as producers, craftsmen, and misunderstood geniuses.

From childhood spaces that cultivated practice and honing their own voice, to early works and projects; their musical influences, those they inspired, and interviews/insights from the fellas themselves - Joyous Invasion brings you a time of honoring two artists who we are so lucky to have had on this earth among us, and the music to carry their spirits long after.

content.text: Barry Mullins is a visual artist, DJ, musician and avid Weather Watcher based in Doolin, County Clare.
"Tune in live to The Weather with Barry Mullins as I
play some of my records to soundtrack what’s going on outside and whats going on inside for you on a Friday morning over a nice coffee and chat."
From his house on a hill over looking the Aran Islands in Doolin, Barry also records his sibling show Sounds From a Hill.

An eclectic mix of music and mutterings, the show has gone on to influence his DJ sets drifting through genres at a whim. Barry is also a member of the DJ collective Sunday Spinners based in North West Clare.

mixcloud_match.name: DJ Gwada Mike - Caribbean Voyage - Éist Radio - Show #7
mixcloud_match.description:
𝐓𝐮𝐧𝐞 𝐢𝐧 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝟕𝐭𝐡 𝐞𝐩𝐢𝐬𝐨𝐝𝐞! 

We're kicking things off with some contemporary jazz, a perfect way to ease into the groove. 

After that, we're throwing it back to the early 2000s and 2010s with some classic Reggae Dancehall, celebrating the influential sounds that defined an era.

From there, we'll move into the present with some vibrant Shatta, before giving a massive shout-out to the incredible musical contributions from the Anglophone islands of the Lesser Antilles. 

Get ready for a high-energy mix of Soca!

Big ups to the scenes of 𝐒𝐨𝐜𝐚, 𝐁𝐨𝐮𝐲𝐨𝐧, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐃𝐞𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐲 𝐒𝐞𝐠𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 + 𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐯𝐢𝐛𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐭 𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐫𝐞𝐬 that get the carnivals grooving. 

With the 𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝗡𝗼𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗛𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗻𝗶𝘃𝗮𝗹 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱 just around the corner in late August, this episode is the perfect way to get in the spirit.

Night Noises features music that feels different late at night. Selecting from different genres, Elinor explores music that captures the feeling of being alone in your bedroom after dark, when the world quiets down, and thoughts grow louder. It’s about solitude, stillness, and the sounds that keep you company when everything else is quiet.

title: C'mere to me with Noreen Murphy

content.text: A Lidl Coffee and a Lidl chat at the Coffee Box.
I've lived in Ballyvolane for over thirty years. I shop in Lidl and I am boring.
A few months ago, a little coffee box opened in the Lidl car park.
I went over and spoke to some of the regulars.
The Coffee Box is attracting customers of all ages and all demographics. Young and not-so-young. School children and politicians. Musicians and pensioners.

Over the last few weeks, I recorded some of the chats I had in the Lidl car park.  The topics cover music, housing, local politics, connection, dogs, changing careers, community, sustainability and the secret to a long and happy marriage.
We hear from future hurling stars, parents, young children, pensioners, a local politician and a lot more besides.
I find out what to wear at a 'Spiderman' birthday and get introduced to the music of Casiopea.
The Coffee Box is not known as a venue but, we have some spoken word and singing from local performers, Anna and Rosie.
Thanks everyone for taking the time to chat with me.
Music by Casiopea, Bruno Mars and John Spillane.
Sub Transmissions is a weekly show focusing on the outer reaches of Ambient, Drone, Bass,Electro, Breaks, Experimental, and everything in between.
This weeks show pretty much straight up Electro .
Slow & deeper at the start & fairly banging toward the end.

An explorative history show - different disciplines, genres, periods and themes of history will be made use of to explore marginalised perspectives and themes of space, time and activity in history. Material history, history of technology, memory studies, contemporary culture, urban geography, archaeology or critical theory are some research areas that would feature. structurally the show would engage with readings, recordings, music (where relevant) and, hopefully at a later stage, interviews. The planned first series is 'On Infrastructure'.

Dip your toe in at Low Tide with ciara_scuro, as she traces strange paths - each track a teaser of much more beneath the surface, getting you excited to dive in to your own curiosity headfirst. Expect unexpected connections, odd facts and, always, a deep love for collective learning and conscious listening.

content.text: Joyous Invasion has traveled by radio waves, birthed from the heart of the southwest desert, to Ireland. Burrowing transcripts of the soul from a time beyond in the hopes of corrupting the strait laced hive minds from the inside out. Dancing past their head chunks that fly in every which way. From the obsessive passions of a POC (Mexican/Zuni/Caddo/Apache/Navajo/Hispanic) New Mexican Burqueña, cartoonist, and filmmaker who hopes to build a community of inclusivity and shared knowledge; a joyous invasion!
We kick off the DOSE Weekender 2025 with a cup of tea and a bit of a yap. Join residents Aloysius and dijaka as we go live from the éist studio with sounds as soft as melted butter.

- Solstice Horoscopes (we do really inaccurate and faux-intelligent horoscope 'readings')

title: Elysia Tuohy - An ear to the liminal

content.text: The show explores the deep connection between Irish mythology and landscape through the lens of queer listening. Embracing ambiguity and non-human perspectives, it invites listeners to engage with both what is heard and what has been historically silenced. Combining field recordings, traditional Irish music fragments, and voice, the piece revisits mythological stories to reconnect with environment and memory amid Ireland’s ongoing ecological and post-colonial challenges. It offers a rich, atmospheric, non-linear sound journey.

mixcloud_match.name: Radio Solstice - Elysia Tuohy - An Ear to the Liminal

content.text: Taking inspiration from Summer Solstice in Japan (Geshi), where in Tokyo, all lights are switched off for the last few hours of daylight. As light moves towards darkness people are encouraged to enjoy slow activities, and candles are lit across the city. Simon explores this moment by playing tracks which reflect the transition; from jazz & kankyō ongaku, to downtempo and ambient techno, helping accompany people through as day turns to night.

title: Katie O'Grady - Palace of Humming Trees

content.text: revisits the collaborative project by artist Jack O’Flynn, writer Maria Sledmere, and curator Katie O’Grady, originally conceived in Glasgow, 2021. The show features Maria’s poetry, read by all three collaborators, weaving a vivid tapestry of human, non-human, and more-than-human states amidst ecological and social unrest. Interspersed with music from their shared mixtapes, featuring Björk, SOPHIE, Nina Simone, Patti Smith, Yves Tumor and others, the programme offers an alchemical, poetic exploration of new mythologies and co-existence.

mixcloud_match.name: Radio Solstice - Katie O'Grady - Palace of Humming Trees

content.text: Tino, a Glaswegian blow-in based in Cork, delivers a new audio documentary exploring Cork, not through facts, but through gossip, misrememberings, and local lore. Building on his 2024 Cork Fringe piece, which captured the river’s ambient spirit, this edition focuses on the funny, the absurd, and the almost-true.
Esmé, field recorder and host of
on Éist, presents an open-format DJ set for

rooted in ambient tribal and field recording traditions. Drawing inspiration from artists like Forest Swords, Muslimgauze and Susumu Yokota, the show explores how traditional sounds can evolve into modern, experimental forms.

title: japhet santana - sonic core of new statues

content.text: This show is inspired by the Aztec earth goddess Coatlicue, the mother of the moon and stars. Like the legendary sculpture of Coatlicue, this mix intends to reveal different faces depending on your perspective.

is a sonic monologue reimagining Cork’s geography as submerged mythology. Created by multidisciplinary artist Jennifer Redmond and poet Matthew Geden, the piece follows a returned emigrant navigating rising floodwaters and vanishing infrastructure. As the digital world dissolves, telepathic ties to his sister beyond Inniscarra emerge: new communication born of crisis. Journeying from the city centre to Knocknaheeny’s higher ground, he sheds the material and awakens the mythic.

'Downstream' is an hour of covers and interpretations (1940s/60s onwards) of old folk standards and ballads. Featuring artists like Karen Dalton, Dave Van Ronk, Ted Hawkins, John Fahey, Mary O'Hara as well as contemporary artists like Lankum, Katy J Pearson with H.Hawkline, Iarla Ó Lionáird, Niamh Bury, the show gathers the songs which have come downstream from generation to generation, bringing with them new interpretations of old mythologies and sentiments.

title: Amy Mac - Rebirth of the Reel

content.text: Clare musician Amy Mac takes us from West Clare to the world, mixing old Irish traditional music with newer interpretations of the classics.

mixcloud_match.name: Radio Solstice - Amy McNamara - Rebirth of the Reel

content.text: Elinor, John, Aisling, and Caoilian are on the decks downstairs at Plugd Records. Elinor wanted to make this a fancy dress party but no one else was that keen. Dress up (if you dare) and come down to Plugd for some wine poured by Brian and a good time all round.

A collective of four artists return to radio with a new improvised audio-theatre piece for Radio Solstice. Following their surreal variety series Celtic Reflections on Éist Radio, this show traces the imagined path of a fictional Irish character on a hero’s journey. Blending interviews with entirely fictitious scholars, artists and passers-by (all played by the collective), the piece unfolds as a playful, atmospheric odyssey through landscape, memory and myth. Built from improvisation and experimental soundscapes, the show draws on Ireland’s storytelling traditions to explore legacy, invention and the creative joy of making it all up as you go.

title: Si Edwards - The Worshippers and The Worshipped

content.text: There is no greater way to acknowledge the influence of a musician or artist than to reinterpret their own works in a new form. This acts to both celebrate the work of one whilst showcasing the adaptability of another, bringing old sounds to new ears in a way that may (or, in some cases, may not) make them more accessible, and also guiding the new initiates towards the wider works from the past. This show will look to unite these factors, featuring versions of classic songs interpreted by other artists, together with the original, lesser heard recordings of songs which have become deeply embedded in popular culture.

mixcloud_match.name: Radio Solstice - Si Edwards - The Worshippers and The Worshipped

—a fictional account of a young cis gay farmer balancing sectoral upheaval while his partners plan an orgy back at the farmhouse. Narrated by Mac Cárthaigh, it explores queer return to rural Ireland against a backdrop of patri-linearity, ecological destruction, and the myth of queer absence.

content.text: is an unveiling of prolific Cork artist Laurie Shaw’s new record of the same name, framed by an interview with his mum. Dreamt before it was written, the album is rooted in Cork City lore, tracing surreal misadventures through familiar streets. Songs like "I Just Pray You're A Licensed Swindler" and "State of the Art Space Shuttle Glass" evoke the anxieties around the current housing crisis while elsewhere "Just Buy The Coat" mythologises the indecision around clothes-buying under the scrutiny of an 'Easter Island Head Peig Sayers'. Inspired by the magnetosphere’s invisible resilience, the album releases on 4th July across streaming platforms.

Recorded at sunrise and sunset, interdisciplinary artist Sarah Partington blends environmental sounds—sea, wind, animals—with spoken word, archival excerpts and experimental instrumentals, tracing the slow, luminous rise of energy across the season.
A pair of chaotic art Hags from the cursed and ancient east threatened to steal our luck and plunder our portion if we didn’t put their show in the schedule. We didn’t want to risk it.

title: dijaka - Still in Search of Solstice

content.text: The celebration of the solstice has existed in Irish traditions since Pagan times, often marked by gathering around the bonfire to share stories, singing and dancing until the rising of the sun. To mark the moment of solstice at 3.41am, dijaka will broadcast live from a secret beach location for a glimpse into the revelry of a contemporary solstice celebration. Who knows what will unfold?

content.text: Pedro's monthly show Farofada is more than just a name – it’s a celebration of mix and blend. In Brazil, farofa is a beloved dish found from north to south, but in Rio de Janeiro, the word also doubles as slang for something chaotic, messy, or working-class. And that’s exactly the spirit behind this show – embracing the full spectrum, from the refined to the raw.

Farofada dives deep into the rich diversity of Brazilian music – from rhythms born in the favelas and outskirts, to sounds that climbed hills, crossed oceans, and resonated around the globe. Rooted traditions meet modern fusions, always seasoned with unmistakable Brazilian flavor.
Get ready to hear a bit of everything – with heart, soul, and joyful disorder. Because in the end, everything becomes farofa. And that’s a compliment

content.text: THE ECLECTIC is a show about taking songs & sounds out of their context, and then putting them back in.

That Got Me To Hear is a whatsapp music sharing project started by Ronan Leonard, where a whatsapp group opens for an hour and people share music and respond to other choices. It opens a few times a week with no regularity. Using his son's DnD dice, Ronan picks a random one and builds an hour long radio show from it.
A monthly show from Alannah Dunford - co-runner of Galway’s esteemed club night and record label, Ar Ais Arís.
Diverse selections of vinyl from all around the World from Damien- music styles , synth , boogie , ambient, 80's , post-punk, house , jazz , disco , wave , balearic.

Deep and soulful house and UK garage from the 90s onwards will be the genres I will focus on mainly but related genres like funk and disco may make an appearance at times most likely early in the show.

content.text: Barry Mullins is a visual artist, DJ, musician and avid Weather Watcher based in Doolin, County Clare.

play some of my records to soundtrack what’s going on outside and whats going on inside for you on a Friday morning over a nice coffee and chat."
From his house on a hill over looking the Aran Islands in Doolin, Barry also records his sibling show Sounds From a Hill.

An eclectic mix of music and mutterings, the show has gone on to influence his DJ sets drifting through genres at a whim. Barry is also a member of the DJ collective Sunday Spinners based in North West Clare.

title: The Chuggernaut Hour with Chuggy Slowtis

content.text: Chuggy Slowtis was living and working happily as a Bavarian sheep herder, until a tragic accident that involved peeing on an electric fence caused such irreparable nerve damage, that meant his sheep herding days were over. (The real tragedy was that it was neither his pee, nor his electric fence, but that’s a story for another day.) Feeling utterly lost and in need of direction, his occupational therapist encouraged him to find a new hobby to help reinvigorate his zest for life. Chuggy took it upon himself to dig through the crates at his local record shop, and unearth records pressed at 45rpm, that sound good at 33rpm. His occupational therapist tried suggesting a slightly less ambitious hobby, but Chuggy would hear none of it. He had found his new calling as the king of wrong speeders (Der König der Falschraser) and is proud to share his finds with the good folk of éist.

Taking inspiration from the pulsating rain and hammering sun, Land of Some Other is an atmospheric vector of high and low pressure patterns. From dry, menacing heat of the desert, to the rainstorms that beckon the spring. In Land of Some Other, your host Christopher Steenson musically traces the turbulence and tranquility of 'frontier', in all its horror and beauty.

Night Noises features music that feels different late at night. Selecting from different genres, Elinor explores music that captures the feeling of being alone in your bedroom after dark, when the world quiets down, and thoughts grow louder. It’s about solitude, stillness, and the sounds that keep you company when everything else is quiet.

Afterglow is a curated exploration of experimental and unconventional music, drawing from a broad range of genres, including left-field club, bass, breaks, ambient, film scores and field recordings. This show highlights different sonic textures and explores music that I don't typically get to play in clubs, while spotlighting both Irish and queer producers. Afterglow focuses on creating a thoughtful, immersive experience that encourages listeners to engage with the unusual and the unexplored.

title: C'mere to me with Noreen Murphy

content.text: Ahead of Refugee Week, I spoke wonderful Cork women, The Rev. Abigail Sines and Dr Manizha Khan about their upcoming event, 'Sanctuary Stories'.
In today's show, we also have music by Martin Leahy.

Refugee Week, 16th to 22nd of June,  is the world’s largest arts and culture festival celebrating the contributions, resilience, and creativity of refugees. Each year, thousands of independently organised events take place globally.

This years theme is 'Community as A SuperPower' and these women embody that theme. We chat about their lives, community, healing, connection, kindness, overcoming challenges and we also chat about dentistry in Ireland.

As their contribution to Refugee Week, they are organising a unique event in Blarney on June 21th.  'Sanctuary Stories'  We will be hearing four inspirational life stories from four inspirational human beings. There will be a craft table for kids and   a tasty lunch and treats! Free of charge!
One hour of instellar funk and soul
This month I invite cee-em-cee aka Cillian. You probably already know him by now. He's one of the soundest and kindest heads in Cork citay and someone who's been supporting me from my baby DJ days.

He is the boss behind the amazing Carmina club night and just recently played at the iconic Sunday Times! This is just naming a few. He loves all things house and bass but his impeccable taste touches all corners of the dance canon.

We're no strangers to playing together and tonight we're bringing you an hour of  smooth groovin' house, tight driving percussions, sweltering synths, with slivers of off-kilter tribal textures and faint echoes of voices just out of reach bringing the story together.

content.text: having fun while meeting our dog’s needs
— through games, training, fitness, play and sport.
We’re joined by two canine professionals,
from Dogsercise, who will tell us  how movement, fitness, and dog sports can….. not just great for canine conditioning — but seriously fun for both you and your pup.
, socialisation, and building a deeper connections between humans and dogs — all while keeping things light, engaging, and safe.
So whether you're tossing a ball, learning new tricks, or diving into dog sports, today is all about making life more joyful — for you and your four-legged  friend.
Shades of all kinds of everything with your host George Kingston and guests.
Multidisciplinary Kenyan artist Labdi Ommes currently stands as one of the few women who plays the orutu professionally, a single-stringed fiddle instrument originating from Kenya.

Labdi will reflect on her artistic journey- from the challenges she faced as an orutu player in a patriarchal society, to claiming back the instrument; her most notable work commissioned by National Geographic Society.

mixcloud_match.name: Caroline Mudingo Dipanda | Transcontinental - Episode 4 Labdi Ommes
mixcloud_match.description:
Labdi Ommes is a brilliant multidisciplinary artist from Kenya. Her practice includes composing, singing-songwriting, visual art, sound installations. Last year, she released her latest EP, Pakruok.
"  Pakruok is about self-praise. This EP is a confirmation and appreciation of oneself. The music takes on  a satirical stance and Labdi addresses different societal issues."


In Kenya, she currently stands as one of the very few women who play the orutu professionally, alongside Omwa Owbara.
The orutu is a single-stringed fiddle originating from Kenya ( Luo people). In this episode Labdi Ommes reflects on her artistic journey - from the challenges she's faced as an orutu player in a patriarchal society, to claiming back the instrument, to preserving the Luo culture. We discuss her notable works including Unganisha, and the music score for award-winning National Geographic Society's documentary Nkashi: Race for the Okavango. 


Nkashi: Race for the Okavango was created by the National Geog

In Kenya, she currently stands as one of the very few woman who plays the orutu professionally, alongside Omwa Owbara.

The orutu is a single-stringed fiddle originating from Kenya ( Luo people). In this episode Labdi Ommes reflects on her artistic journey - from the challenges she's faced as an orutu player in a patriarchal society, to claiming back the instrument, to preserving the Luo culture.
