content.text: A monthly show from Alannah Dunford - co-runner of Galway’s esteemed club night and record label, Ar Ais Arís.
Dreamscape episode beginning with an interview clip from composer Angelo Badalamenti explaining how himself and David Lynch conjured up the synth sounds of the Twin Peaks soundtrack.
Ending with Roy Orbison's 'In Dreams', an ode to David Lynch.
A mix of new and old experimental, avant-garde, etc sounds, with occasional guest mixes from artists, label operators, and avid listeners from the outer fringes.

content.text: AUDIO-ESSAY, POETRY, INTERVIEW, & MUSIC CENTRED AROUND MIRROR WORLDS.
THE ECLECTIC is a show about taking songs & sounds out of their context, and then putting them back in.

That Got Me To Hear is a whatsapp music sharing project started by Ronan Leonard, where a whatsapp group opens for an hour and people share music and respond to other choices. It opens a few times a week with no regularity. Using his son's DnD dice, Ronan picks a random one and builds an hour long radio show from it.
Diverse selections of vinyl from all around the World from Damien- music styles , synth , boogie , ambient, 80's , post-punk, house , jazz , disco , wave , balearic.

title: C'mere to Me with Noreen Murphy

content.text: , I speak to my former colleague,
Broadcaster and Blackpool resident, Fahmeda, works in the areas of EDI & Belonging, human rights, activism, peace Interagency, communities ,Intercultural communications, equality and equity.
She is an independent researcher and a Community Development Officer and passionate to work for equal societies.
Fahmeda is a member of the Network of Ethnic Minority Domestic Abuse and Gender Violence, and is an ambassador of Schools of Sanctuary.
Cork Civic awardee for 2024 and  founder of the Irish Pakistani Community of Cork.

Lord Mayor Community Award winner 2023, National Diversity Award Winner, Intercultural Epic Award winner, Community Service Award winner, All Women Global Award Winner ,shortlisted award winner of Exceptional Endeavour Award 2024, roll of honoured for Exceptional Endeavour Award 2023 and others.
Fahmeda presents and produces her radio show, ‘Let’s Integrate’ on Cork City Community Radio.
As if that’s not enough, she is a tutor for Fusion Cooking Course, is a writer, peot and wonderful human. and I am so privileged to know her.
After the break, we have some music from American band,

Originally formed as a one off party band, they continue to carry that mantle to this day. They have shared stages with the Detroit Cobras, Tav Falco and Panther Burns, John Sinclair, Low Cut Connie and Cedric Burnside. Mixing genres that are typically disparate, such as blues, psych, classic rock and jazz, Thee All-Seeing Eyes have kept audiences on edge by taking unorthodox approaches that are unpredictable and exploratory.

Hailing from the small river town of Dayton KY, the 3 multi-instrumentalists have crafted their new record “Trone des Fleur” (Masonic Records) and are ready to present it to the world. The tracks were all arranged, recorded, and produced by the band at the studio that they built by hand, Masonic Sounds Studio B.

Johnny Walker is a board certified psychiatrist specializing in psychosis and mania. This bleeds through into his guitar playing, as he believes playing live music is a form of therapy for the masses. Being Soledad Brother #1, he has played his own brand of psych/blues/rock for hundreds of thousands of music fans. Dr Walker has played all the slide guitar on the White Stripes self titled first album. He sang for the MC5 on their first European tour in 30 years in 2004. He even backed up John Sinclair in numerous shows while the Chief read prose about blues guitar players. Johnny was asked to reform the Detroit Cobras, playing bass for them while in medical school. He has engaged in many recording sessions including The Greenhornes, Low Cut Connie and too many more to list here. Johnny’s band the Soledad Brothers, was noted to be John Peel's favorite band, per John’s son. Mr Peel gave them the honor of being the first track on his only curated compilation, Fabric Live, serving as bookends for the comp with the Undertones. That is high praise, indeed.  Third Man Records (Jack White's record label) will also be releasing all the back catalog of Soledad Brothers material in the near future.

Lance Kaufman is a drummer and vocalist extraordinaire. He has fronted traditional rock billy band, the Star Devils for decades. He was the principal engineer for the new Thee All-Seeing Eyes album as well. While holding down the bass duties in the band, Kane Kitchen has done dozens of sessions at Masonic Sounds Studios and toured extensively with outlaw country star Jeremy Pinnell. He has displayed the ability to adapt to numerous genres of music and is a great arranger of songs.
You can get a chance to see them live in Ireland this summer,  don't miss them or
Fri 4th July-The Glens Centre (Manorhamilton, Co. Leitrim)
Cork Fringe x éist HOMECOMING: Northside / Southside

Northside / Southside is a comedic and heartfelt radio show that delves into the contrasting lives of two Cork natives—one who stayed, and one who left. Through lively banter, character-driven sketches, and traditional music, we explore the essence of ‘Homecoming’ and what it means to reconnect with one’s roots. It’s a celebration of Cork’s rich culture, humour, and the enduring bonds of friendship.

title: HOMECOMING: Leafy Garments - River Lee Special

content.text: Cork Fringe x éist HOMECOMING: Leafy Garments - River Lee Special

The River Lee is a magical place. Herons, seals, cormorants, tidal waterfalls and beaches exist alongside record shops, cafes, cargo ships and extensive traffic jams. The city is both encircled by the river and also revolves around it. On the walk from the Port of Cork on the east of the city, through the bustling city centre, and out to the leafy and wild Lee Fields we take a wander and chat to the locals to find out their favourite things about Cork in 2025.

title: HOMECOMING: the women who cried for a year (and counting)

content.text: Cork Fringe x éist HOMECOMING: the women who cried for a year (and counting)

A Cork woman claws through inherited grief and her mother’s shadow in the city’s damp streets. Fragmented voice notes, tarot card whispers, and the Lee’s choked currents frame her raw question: Can a homecoming mend fractures deepened by generations? It’s a visceral ode to survival in the city that raised and ruptured her.

content.text: A fever-dream of Country ‘n Irish music that descends into a dub-tank collage-history of the music, the people, the dreams and nightmares. Sampled, deconstructed and processed, each episode confronts the strange memories of this misunderstood music. Below the kitsch surface lies a deep, generative weirdness, which this series will attempt to extrapolate and re-integrate. Exploring the outer reaches of Country n’ Irish, this show will try to uncover something about the dark heart of Irish nationhood through a deep analysis of an endlessly strange and culturally promiscuous genre. Each episode will find echoes in unexpected places, times and musics; its tendrils reaching out across history and geography. As such, it will be more of a delirious, erratic talking cure than methodical history.

content.text: REVEIL embarks on its 12th loop around the sun on Saturday 3rd of May at 5am UTC+1 and will complete its journey on Sunday the 4th of May at 6am.
Reveil is a collaborative sound and radio project that circles the Earth on live audio streams at daybreak. The last broadcast featured 145 streams from a wide range of environments and situations.
This radio stream is presented in partnership with Wave Farm in New York's Upper Hudson Valley, USA.

Wave Farm is a non-profit arts organization driven by experimentation with broadcast media and the airwaves. A pioneer of the Transmission Arts genre, Wave Farm provides access to transmission technologies and supports artists and organizations that engage with media and the electromagnetic spectrum as an art form.

content.text: Late night transmissions to the deep. .  .   .   .    .

Joyous Invasion has traveled by radio waves, birthed from the heart of the southwest desert, to Ireland. Burrowing transcripts of the soul from a time beyond in the hopes of corrupting the strait laced hive minds from the inside out. Dancing past their head chunks that fly in every which way. From the obsessive passions of a Burqueña, cartoonist, and filmmaker who hopes to build a community of inclusivity and shared knowledge; a joyous invasion!

title: Sound Atlas - guest dj mixes from around the Globe - Phil Mison

content.text: Phil Mison is a highly respected figure in the world of Balearic music, known for his laid-back, sun-drenched sounds and influential DJ sets. Originally from London, Mison began his career in the early 1990s as a DJ, gaining significant attention through residencies at the celebrated London club
and later becoming a pivotal part of the Ibiza scene.

A chance meeting with José Padilla, the legendary Café del Mar resident DJ, helped launch Mison’s international career. Padilla invited him to play at Café del Mar, where Mison honed his skills in crafting atmospheric, genre-blending sets that perfectly captured the spirit of the Balearic Islands.
In the early 2000s, Mison started producing music under the name
. The project became synonymous with beautifully melodic, global-tinged chillout music, blending elements of lounge, world music, and ambient styles. His self-titled debut album
(2003) became an instant classic within the Balearic and downtempo communities, featuring lush instrumentation, tropical rhythms, and a serene, cinematic feel.
Over the years, Mison has released several acclaimed Cantoma albums, including:
His sound often features collaborations with singers and instrumentalists from around the world, and his music is a staple on chillout and lounge playlists globally.

Aside from his Cantoma work, Mison continues to DJ internationally, maintaining his reputation as a master of the Balearic style—effortlessly blending house, ambient, world music, and soul influences.

Phil Mison's music under the Cantoma name captures a feeling of escapism, often described as the soundtrack to idyllic summer days and beach sunsets. Drawing inspiration from world cultures, vintage exotica, early electronic music, and classic Balearic sounds, his work is noted for its warmth, timelessness, and emotional resonance.

title: Shakespearean Drama with The Cork Shakespearean Company

content.text: Cork Shakespearean Company presents William Shakespeare's Macbeth.

Macbeth is successful in battle and lauded by the people, and the King. He encounters three witches who promise him success and prosperity beyond his imagining - but is this prophecy a blessing or a curse?
This classic bloody tragedy is brought to life by the performances of the Cork Shakespearean Company, directed by Kieran O' Leary and with sound recording and production by Sarah Kelly.
Many thanks to Rebel Reads for their help in recording, and to Éist Radio for hosting.
Cork Shakespearean Company meets every Thursday at 20:00 in the Rock Community Centre, Blarney Street, and all are welcome to join us,

soundcloud_match.title: Ellen O'Keeffe - Ellen O'Keeffe - Beetle, Bramble and Blackbird: Near-by Nature 06.04.25

content.text: A fever-dream of Country ‘n Irish music that descends into a dub-tank collage-history of the music, the people, the dreams and nightmares. Sampled, deconstructed and processed, each episode confronts the strange memories of this misunderstood music. Below the kitsch surface lies a deep, generative weirdness, which this series will attempt to extrapolate and re-integrate. Exploring the outer reaches of Country n’ Irish, this show will try to uncover something about the dark heart of Irish nationhood through a deep analysis of an endlessly strange and culturally promiscuous genre. Each episode will find echoes in unexpected places, times and musics; its tendrils reaching out across history and geography. As such, it will be more of a delirious, erratic talking cure than methodical history.

content.text: Yuvraj Khatri, vice president of CNC took time out to talk to us about their rich culture and upcoming Nepalese New Year event. After the break, we catch up with Paul O'Riordan and Judith Haywood from greyhound rescue Great Hounds in Need.

content.text: The “Château de Sibra”, nestled in the heart of the Cathare country (Ariège, F), is a former stronghold which was remodelled some 150 years ago by Joseph and Pauline Villary de Fajac, surrounded by a vast English-style park, bathed in romanticism. In the heart of the park, the „Manoir Bruit“ - a sculpture in form of a tiny house by Haus am Gern - will welcome sound artists from Biel/Bienne (CH) over the next four years.

Joyous Invasion has traveled by radio waves, birthed from the heart of the southwest desert, to Ireland. Burrowing transcripts of the soul from a time beyond in the hopes of corrupting the strait laced hive minds from the inside out. Dancing past their head chunks that fly in every which way.
A tribute to the late great Mr. Sunshine - Samples and Remix special.

content.text: Continuing on this month where we ended last month we begin in Algeria before moving through Morocco and on to the Sahara stopping in Mauritania, Mali and Niger. From Niger we head to Senegal and then on to Sierra Leone, Cote d'Ivoire and back to Ghana before travelling to Sao Tome and Principe and the Cape Verde Islands. The last half hour of this months show we will be remembering Lee Scratch Perry and William Onyeabor who both would have celebrated their birthdays this month.
Join us to catch to catch a flavour of what to expect ahead of our show in Dali with Hyperdawn, Mantua and Boy Lucid tonight.

content.text: Get ready for a musical exploration with DJ Gwada Mike.
He'll be showcasing the vibrant sounds of the Caribbean, World, and Urban music scenes, and highlighting the artists and labels that have left an indelible mark on music and the global scenes.
An audio essay about tech capital, endless scrolling, fragmented sound, MIDI, medieval music, text to speech, dungeon synth, autotune, drone technology and financialisation.

content.text: European 7-inch singles which never made it to Irish radio before, oddball cover versions, music from the rich musical fields of Africa, Brazil and Japan which still sound as fresh as ever forty years later.

content.text: A fever-dream of Country ‘n Irish music that descends into a dub-tank collage-history of the music, the people, the dreams and nightmares. Sampled, deconstructed and processed, each episode confronts the strange memories of this misunderstood music. Below the kitsch surface lies a deep, generative weirdness, which this series will attempt to extrapolate and re-integrate. Exploring the outer reaches of Country n’ Irish, this show will try to uncover something about the dark heart of Irish nationhood through a deep analysis of an endlessly strange and culturally promiscuous genre. Each episode will find echoes in unexpected places, times and musics; its tendrils reaching out across history and geography. As such, it will be more of a delirious, erratic talking cure than methodical history.

content.text: I'll be playing records from all over the world over the next few months. Shades of all kinds of everything with your host George Kingston and guests.
A music show focusing primarily on black music, and afro-diasporic stories. The show aims to offer a glimpse into an era, a country; to place an artist and music in context.

mixcloud_match.name: Caroline Mudingo Dipanda - Transcontinental: Aby Ngana Diop
mixcloud_match.description:
The programme explores the unprecedented recording of griotte Aby Ngana Diop, who performed taasu over mbalax music. " The combination of Diop and her backup vocalists with their powerful taasu, along with the driving, complex rhythms of the sabar and tama drums, mixed with key elements of mbalax (such as the syncopated, polyphonic marimba sounds played on the Yamaha DX7 keyboard) was something the Senegalese public had never heard before" P.Tang

Guests:
Marame Gueye is Associate Professor of African and African Diaspora literatures in the department of English at East Carolina in Greenville, NC. 

ABA DIOP is a Senegalese master sabar percussionist, vocalist and composer who comes from a lineage of griots (oral historians, poets, storytellers) . 

Amadou Bator Dieng is a journalist who worked as a correspondent for Pan African Music( PAM) in Dakar, Senegal.

soundcloud_match.title: Caroline Mudingo Dipanda - Transcontinental - In focus: Aby Ngana Diop
soundcloud_match.description:
The programme explores the unprecedented recording of griotte Aby Ngana Diop, who performed taasu over mbalax music ( "taasu is a form of oral poetry spoken to the rhythmic accompaniment of sabar and tama drums [...]It is a rhythmically declaimed speech in call-and-response with a small chorus of female vocalists'' Patricia Tang for Awesome Tapes From Africa). She released her only album in 1994 in Senegal, which Awesome Tapes From Africa distributed in 2014.

"The combination of Diop and her backup vocalists with their powerful taasu, along with the driving, complex rhythms of the sabar and tama drums, mixed with key elements of mbalax (such as the syncopated, polyphonic marimba sounds played on the Yamaha DX7 keyboard) was something the Senegalese public had never heard before" - P.Tang

Guests:

Marame Gueye is Associate Professor of African and African Diaspora literatures in the department of English at East Carolina in Greenville, NC.
Her research interests include African women’s art, African oral literatures, translation and immigration studies. Her articles have appeared in Research in African Literatures, African Studies Review, the Journal of Pan African Studies.

ABA DIOP is a Senegalese master sabar percussionist, vocalist and composer who comes from a lineage of griots ( griot: musicians, oral historians, poets, storytellers) . Aba Diop & the Yermande Family is a music ensemble that consists of Aba Diop (sabar, vocals), Noumoucounda Cissoko (kora, vocals), Samba Ndokh (tama), and Jason Hosier (electric guitar).

Amadou Bator Dieng is the founder of  Kirinapost, a pan-African newspaper. He worked as a correspondent for Pan African Music( PAM) in Dakar, Senegal. PAM is an online music magazine in both French and English, dedicated to promoting music from the African continent and diaspora.

content.text: I’m using the concept of the magic hill as a visual metaphor for the journey of the show. The first half is chill as we descend down into position at the bottom of the hill and the second half is uplifting as we strangely roll up the hill. Emotional pensive music across listed genres. Expansive, calming, restorative and strange, stimulating and invigorating much like the feeling of letting the handbrake off and rolling up the magic hill.

content.text: People turn to poetry to find expression, be consoled, grieve or rage, remember, and even reanimate the dead. The poem can also be a powerful critical tool for cutting through political reverberations that hang over and distort the events that constitute our reality. In The Expanded Field, strategies of poetic creation are debated and extended by audio effects. Aesthetic and ethical boundaries are pushed out and beyond the page. With Jennifer Redmond & Matthew Geden.

content.text: An hour’s worth of music drawing from a WhatsApp group chat between (all kinds of) music lovers on 10 November 2024.

title: Slow Discoid Funk For Lonely Space Travellers

content.text: Vinyl , roughly soul, hip-hop, reggae, blues, disco, Latin, house, new-wave, electro

soundcloud_match.title: JusMe - Slow Discoid Funk For Lonely Space Travellers (17/02/25)
soundcloud_match.description:
It’s a little mix i made for Rustie, a space traveller friend of mine. Hes currently 2 years into a 3 year mission. Its important work hes doing, exploring the cosmos, but he gets lonely up there. Misses his family, friends, cold beer etc.
He does have a colleague with him. Dale.. He likes Dale. Deeply respects him. Dale’s quick thinking had saved both thier lives more than once. But after a couple years on the ship they’ve run out of things to say to each other. They prefer listening to music these days. They even have an occasional dance to lighten the mood, although the lack of gravity means they move slowly and so prefer songs with slower tempos. 
I quite liked how the mix of slow, off kilter, dubby, slightly sad, spacey disco tunes came out so decided to share with the rest of you.

content.text: Deep House Work is a radio show all about focus, getting sh*t done, getting in to a state of flow while listening to great music.

The goal of the show is to help people be more intentional with their focus and goals. The show combines prompts/quotes/thoughts of the day etc and deep house music to help people achieve what they want to in a two hour block of deep work.

content.text: Barry Mullins is a visual artist, DJ, musician and avid Weather Watcher based in Doolin, County Clare.
"The Weather is a big part of peoples lives on this little island, especially out west where talk of rain and wind is as common as an
Tune into The Weather with Barry Mullins as I
play some of my records to soundtrack what’s going on outside."
From his house on a hill over looking the Aran Islands in Doolin, Barry also records his sibling show Sounds From a Hill.

An eclectic mix of music and mutterings, the show has gone on to influence his DJ sets drifting through genres at a whim. Barry is also a member of the DJ collective Sunday Spinners based in North West Clare.

content.text: Examining Irish society and culture to understand how our history, psychology, and politics have shaped what it means to be Irish today.

Join me as I host conversations with Irish people who are experts in different facets of Irish culture, including nature, language, healing, folklore, mythology, music, poetry, mental health, entrepreneurism, activism, identity, social issues and current affairs.
I also ask every guest what it means to be Irish. Their answers may surprise you.

content.text: Pat Falvey is a world-record breaking Irish explorer, having been the first person to climb the highest Seven Summits of the world, twice! His journey starts from a council house in the Northside of Cork City, where he watched planes taking off and landing in Cork airport. He was a dreamer who went on to achieve his dreams such as climbing Mount Everest, leading great expeditions to some of the most extreme corners of our globe and learning life's most important lessons from the remotest of tribes.

In this episode, Pat talks about growing up as a Norrie, going on to become a millionaire aged just 20, to losing it all and becoming depressed aged 29. He explains how climbing Mount Carrauntoohil in County Kerry saved his life, as it gave him a new goal and motivation - to reach the summit of the highest mountain of them all, Mount Everest.
Pat explains what he has learned from his achievements and his failures and how being Irish has been an important part of it all.
Follow Pat and his adventures over on patfalvey.com, or @patfalveyexplorer on Instagram and YouTube.

content.text: Pat Falvey is a world-record breaking Irish explorer, having been the first person to climb the highest Seven Summits of the world, twice! His journey starts from a council house in the Northside of Cork City, where he watched planes taking off and landing in Cork airport. He was a dreamer who went on to achieve his dreams such as climbing Mount Everest, leading great expeditions to some of the most extreme corners of our globe and learning life's most important lessons from the remotest of tribes.

In this episode, Pat talks about growing up as a Norrie, going on to become a millionaire aged just 20, to losing it all and becoming depressed aged 29. He explains how climbing Mount Carrauntoohil in County Kerry saved his life, as it gave him a new goal and motivation - to reach the summit of the highest mountain of them all, Mount Everest.
Pat explains what he has learned from his achievements and his failures and how being Irish has been an important part of it all.
Follow Pat and his adventures over on patfalvey.com, or @patfalveyexplorer on Instagram and YouTube.

title: Beetle, bramble and blackbird: Near-by Nature

content.text: Exploring the diverse people and groups in and around Cork who are doing interesting and hope-giving projects, actions, and work around biodiversity, climate action, nature connection, ecological and nature friendly farming, forest school and much more.

There are so many people doing heaps of wonderful things with and for the living world. I'm so excited to share interviews with them here. You'll hear from lots of groups you could follow and join, and hopefully there'll be ideas of actions you can take in your own life to become more connected to the living world around you.

content.text: A fever-dream of Country ‘n Irish music that descends into a dub-tank collage-history of the music, the people, the dreams and nightmares. Sampled, deconstructed and processed, each episode confronts the strange memories of this misunderstood music. Below the kitsch surface lies a deep, generative weirdness, which this series will attempt to extrapolate and re-integrate. Exploring the outer reaches of Country n’ Irish, this show will try to uncover something about the dark heart of Irish nationhood through a deep analysis of an endlessly strange and culturally promiscuous genre. Each episode will find echoes in unexpected places, times and musics; its tendrils reaching out across history and geography. As such, it will be more of a delirious, erratic talking cure than methodical history.

Exploring the diverse people and groups in and around Cork who are doing interesting and hope-giving projects, actions, and work around biodiversity, climate action, nature connection, ecological and nature friendly farming, forest school and much more. There are so many people doing heaps of wonderful things with and for the living world. I'm so excited to share interviews with them here. You'll hear from lots of groups you could follow and join, and hopefully there'll be ideas of actions you can take in your own life to become more connected to the living world around you.
